volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 1.1.137 - Section 1.4.228)

sections.label: Section 1.1.137
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.137b
sections.original: Redeo inde iratus atque aegre ferens .
sections.translation: I returned thence in anger , and hurt at heart : and yet there was not sufficient ground for reproving him . He might say ; What have I done ? How have I deserved this , or offended , father ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.140
sections.original: Quae sese in ignem iniicere voluit , prohibui ,
sections.translation: She who wished to throw herself into the flames , I prevented ; I saved her . The defense is a reasonable one .

sections.label: Section 1.1.141b
sections.translation: You judge aright ; for if you censure him who has assisted to preserve life , what are you to do to him who causes loss or misfortune to it ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.144
sections.original: Venit Chremes postridie ad me clamitans ,
sections.translation: Chremes comes to me next day , exclaiming :

sections.label: Section 1.1.145
sections.translation: " Disgraceful conduct !"— that he had ascertained that Pamphilus was keeping this foreign woman as a wife . I steadfastly denied that to be the fact . He insisted that it was the fact . In short , I then left him refusing to bestow his daughter .

sections.label: Section 1.1.149b
sections.translation: Did not you then reprove your son ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.149c
sections.translation: Not even this

sections.label: Section 1.1.150
sections.translation: was a cause sufficiently strong for censuring him .

sections.label: Section 1.1.150b
sections.translation: How so ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.151
sections.original: Tute ipse his rebus finem praescripsti pater .
sections.translation: You yourself , father , he might say , have prescribed a limit to these proceedings . The time is near , when I must live according to the humor of another ; meanwhile , for the present allow me to live according to my own .

sections.label: Section 1.1.154
sections.original: Qui igitur relictus est obiurgandi locus ?
sections.translation: What room for reproving him , then , is there left ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.155
sections.original: Si propter amorem uxorem nolit ducere ,
sections.translation: If on account of his amour he shall decline to take a wife , that , in the first place , is an offense on his part to be censured . And now for this am I using my endeavors , that , by means of the pretended marriage , there may be real ground for rebuking him , if he should refuse ; at the same time , that if that rascal Davus has any scheme ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.160
sections.original: Habet ut consumat nunc cum nihil obsint doli ;
sections.translation: he may exhaust it now , while his knaveries can do no harm : who , I do believe , with hands , feet , and all his might , will do every thing ; and more for this , no doubt , that he may do me an ill turn , than to oblige my son .

sections.label: Section 1.1.163b
sections.translation: For what reason ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.163c
sections.translation: Do you ask ? Bad heart , bad disposition . Whom , however , if I do detect —

sections.label: Section 1.1.165
sections.original: Sed quid opus est verbis ? Sin eveniat quod volo ,
sections.translation: But what need is there of talking ? If it should turn out , as I wish , that there is no delay on the part of Pamphilus , Chremes remains to be prevailed upon by me ; and I do hope that all will go well . Now it ’ s your duty to pretend these nuptials cleverly , to terrify Davus ; and watch my son ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.170
sections.original: Quid agat , quid cum illo consili captet .
sections.translation: what he ’ s about , what schemes he is planning with him .

sections.label: Section 1.1.170b
sections.translation: ’ Tis enough ; I ’ ll take care ; now let ’ s go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 1.1.171b
sections.translation: You go first ; I ’ ll follow .

sections.label: Section 1.2.172
sections.original: Non dubium est quin uxorem nolit filius :
sections.translation: There ’ s no doubt but that my son doesn ’ t wish for a wife ; so alarmed did I perceive Davus to be just now , when he heard that there was going to be a marriage . But the very man is coming out of the house . ( Stands aside .)

sections.label: Section 1.2.175
sections.original: Mirabar hoc si sic abiret , et heri semper lenitas
sections.translation: I was wondering if this matter was to go off thus ; and was continually dreading where my master ’ s good humor would end ; for , after he had heard that a wife would not be given to his son , he never uttered a word to any one of us , or took it amiss .

sections.label: Section 1.2.179
sections.original: At nunc faciet ; neque , ut opinor , sine tuo magno malo .
sections.translation: But now he ’ ll do so : and that , I fancy , not without heavy cost to you .

sections.label: Section 1.2.180
sections.original: Id voluit , nos sic nec opinantes duci falso gaudio ,
sections.translation: He meant this , that we , thus unsuspecting , should be led away by delusive joy ; that now in hope , all fear being removed , we might during our supineness be surprised , so that there might be no time for planning a rupture of the marriage . How clever !

sections.label: Section 1.2.183b
sections.translation: The villain ! what does he say ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.183c
sections.translation: It ’ s my master , and I didn ’ t see him .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184
sections.translation: Davus .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184b
sections.translation: Well , what is it ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.184c
sections.translation: Just step this way to me .

sections.label: Section 1.2.184d
sections.translation: What does he want ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.184e
sections.translation: What are you saying ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.184f
sections.translation: About what ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.184g
sections.translation: Do you ask the question ? There ’ s a report that my son ’ s in love .

sections.label: Section 1.2.185b
sections.translation: The public troubles itself about that , [*] of course .

sections.label: Section 1.2.186
sections.translation: Will you attend to this , or not ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.186b
sections.translation: Certainly , I will , to that .

sections.label: Section 1.2.186c
sections.translation: But for me to inquire now into these matters , were the part of a severe father . For what he has done hitherto , doesn ’ t concern me at all . So long as his time of life prompted to that course , I allowed him to indulge his inclination : now this day brings on another mode of life , demands other habits .

sections.label: Section 1.2.190
sections.original: Dehinc postulo , sive aequum est te oro , Dave , ut redeat iam in viam .
sections.translation: From this time forward , I do request , or if it is reasonable , I do entreat you , Davus , that he may now return to the right path .

sections.label: Section 1.2.191
sections.translation: What can this mean ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.191b
sections.original: Omnes qui amant graviter sibi dari uxorem ferunt .
sections.translation: All who are intriguing take it ill to have a wife given them .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192
sections.translation: So they say .

sections.label: Section 1.2.192b
sections.original: Tum siquis magistrum cepit ad eam rem improbum
sections.translation: And if anyone has adopted a bad instructor in that course , he generally urges the enfeebled mind to pursuits still more unbecoming .

sections.label: Section 1.2.194
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I do not comprehend .

sections.label: Section 1.2.194b
sections.translation: No ? Ha —

sections.label: Section 1.2.194c
sections.translation: No — I am Davus , not Oedipus . [*]

sections.label: Section 1.2.195
sections.original: Nempe ergo aperte vis quae restant me loqui .
sections.translation: Of course then , you wish me to speak plainly in what further I have to say .

sections.label: Section 1.2.195b
sections.translation: Certainly , by all means .

sections.label: Section 1.2.196
sections.original: Si sensero hodie quicquam in his te nuptiis
sections.translation: If I this day find out that you are attempting any trickery about this marriage , to the end that it may not take place ; or are desirous that in this matter it should be proved how knowing you are ; I ’ ll hand you over , Davus , beaten with stripes , to the mill , [*] even to your dying day ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.200
sections.original: Ea lege atque omine , ut si te inde exemerim ego pro te molam .
sections.translation: upon this condition and pledge , that if ever I release you , I shall grind in your place . Now , do you understand this ? Or not yet even this ?

sections.label: Section 1.2.201b
sections.translation: Yes , perfectly : you have now spoken so plainly upon the subject , you have not used the least circumlocution .

sections.label: Section 1.2.203
sections.original: Ubi vis facilius passus sim , quam in hac re , me deludier .
sections.translation: In any thing would I more willingly allow myself to be imposed upon than in this matter .

sections.label: Section 1.2.204
sections.translation: Fair words , I entreat .

sections.label: Section 1.2.204b
sections.original: Irrides : nil me fallis . Edico tibi
sections.translation: You are ridiculing me : you don ’ t at all deceive me . I give you warning ,

sections.label: Section 1.2.205
sections.original: Ne temere facias : neque tu hoc dicas tibi non praedictum . Cave .
sections.translation: don ’ t act rashly , and don ’ t say you were not warned . Take care .

sections.label: Section 1.3.206
sections.original: Enimvero , Dave , nil loci est segnitiae neque socordiae ,
sections.translation: Assuredly , Davus , there ’ s no room for slothfulness or inactivity , so far as I ’ ve just now ascertained the old man ’ s mind about the marriage ; which if it is not provided against by cunning , will be bringing either myself or my master to ruin . What to do , I am not determined ; whether I should assist Pamphilus or obey the old man .

sections.label: Section 1.3.210
sections.original: Si illum relinquo eius vitae timeo : sin opitulor huius minas ;
sections.translation: If I desert the former , I fear for his life ; if I assist him , I dread the other ’ s threats , on whom it will be a difficult matter to impose . In the first place , he has now found out about this amour ; with hostile feelings he watches me , lest I should be devising some trickery against the marriage . If he discovers it , I ’ m undone ; or even if he chooses to allege any pretext , whether rightfully or wrongfully , he will consign me headlong to the mill .

sections.label: Section 1.3.215
sections.original: Ad haec mala hoc mihi accedit etiam : haec Andria ,
sections.translation: To these evils this one is besides added for me . This Andrian , whether she is his wife , or whether his mistress , is pregnant by Pamphilus . It is worth while to hear their effrontery ; for it is an undertaking worthy of those in their dotage , not of those who dote in love ; [*] whatever she shall bring forth , they have resolved to rear ; [*]

sections.label: Section 1.3.220
sections.original: Et fingunt quandam inter se nunc fallaciam ,
sections.translation: and they are now contriving among themselves a certain scheme , that she is a citizen of Attica . There was formerly a certain old man of this place , a merchant ; he was shipwrecked off the Isle of Andros ; he died . They say that there , the father of Chrysis , on that occasion , sheltered this girl , thrown on shore , an orphan , a little child . What nonsense !

sections.label: Section 1.3.225
sections.original: Mihi quidem hercle non fit verisimile ; atque ipsis commentum placet .
sections.translation: To myself at least it isn ’ t very probable ; the fiction pleases them , however . But Mysis is coming out of the house . Now I ’ ll betake myself hence to the Forum , [*] that I may meet with Pamphilus , lest his father should take him by surprise about this matter .

sections.label: Section 1.4.228
sections.original: Audivi Archylis iamdudum : Lesbiam adduci iubes .
sections.translation: I ’ ve heard you already , Archylis ; you request Lesbia to be fetched . Really , upon my faith , she is a wine-bibbing [*] and a rash woman ,
